rationale: Both titles are nearly identical and strong overall. Y improves on X in two specific ways: (1) 'AV Block Risk' is more precise than 'AV Block' alone — the study reports incidence/risk of AV block as a complication, not an instance of the arrhythmia itself, so the added word corrects a subtle inaccuracy; (2) 'NCDR IMPACT Registry' is the correct full registry name, adding specificity that aids readers in identifying the data source and signals methodological rigor. These are small but substantive improvements in informativeness and journal fit for a JACC readership that values precise, registry-anchored language.
loser_weakness: X omits 'Risk' from 'AV Block', which slightly misrepresents the study focus (risk/incidence of AV block as a complication vs. the arrhythmia itself), and truncates the registry name to 'IMPACT Registry' without the 'NCDR' prefix, reducing identifiability and precision.
